title: Jojoba and vitamin E facial serum
subtitle: A lightweight three-oil serum for daily facial moisturising and antioxidant protection
excerpt: Jojoba is technically a liquid wax ester, structurally close to skin sebum. Rosehip adds high-linoleic-acid hydration; vitamin E extends the shelf life and adds antioxidant activity. Two to three drops cover the entire face. Use morning or evening under moisturiser.
sourceNotes: Jojoba oil composition and compatibility with skin: Pazyar et al., Iranian Journal of Dermatology (2013). Rosehip oil linoleic acid content: Gruenwald et al., 2007 referenced in Botanical Pharmacognosy. Vitamin E as an antioxidant in oil preparations: cosmetic formulation standards.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep batches small as rosehip oil has a short shelf life.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in 30 ml batches; the rosehip oil limits shelf life to two to three months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within three months. Refrigerate to extend life. Discard if the serum develops a fishy or rancid smell -- rosehip oil oxidises and the serum becomes comedogenic when rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: Technically a liquid wax ester from the jojoba shrub, not a true oil. Structurally similar to human skin sebum. Very long shelf life; nut-free; absorbs quickly without greasiness.

content.text: as the base (its feel is close to skin's own oil), rosehip for its skin-barrier fatty acids, and vitamin E as an antioxidant. Rose geranium adds a light floral scent. The serum absorbs in a minute or two and sits well under SPF or moisturiser.

items.prepNote: about 2.5 ml; acts as an antioxidant

content.text: Combine the jojoba oil, rosehip oil, and vitamin E oil in the dropper bottle. Add the essential oil if using. Cap and shake gently to mix. The serum is a simple blend of oils -- no emulsification required.

Apply two to three drops to clean, slightly damp skin. Warm between the palms and press gently onto the face and neck. Allow to absorb for one to two minutes before applying SPF or moisturiser on top. Suitable for morning and evening use. Patch test on the inside of the wrist before first facial use.

The jojoba base is non-comedogenic and suits most skin types including oily. Rosehip oil is high in linoleic acid, which makes it particularly suitable for acne-prone and combination skin. For very dry skin, substitute sweet almond oil for the jojoba.

Keep in the amber dropper bottle, away from light and heat. Refrigerate to extend the life of the rosehip oil. Use within three months. A rancid, fishy smell means the rosehip oil has oxidised and the serum should be discarded -- oxidised oils increase the risk of breakouts.
